messages.text:
Preston Elmer 
Have you reached out to TG for a call in the morning?
 
 
Itzel Chavez 
Friday night issues with TG 4& 5
I am not sure how many molds it takes to fill the gondola, only run enough bars to fill the gondola + 50 more molds.
 
Send 1/2 to TG 4 then 1/2 to TG 5.
 
Empty the WDS. 
 
I reached out to Matt asking if he could speak to a TG tech so a tech can call Preston in the morning. 
 
 
If you could reach out to Felix or any other tech you have a number for that would be great.
 
 
If a TG tech responds post here, so we know what time someone can help us.
 
I will be in early so we can go over the plan, or if you have a different idea please let me know.
 
 
 
I hope you had a wonderful Mother's Day ☺️

Thank you for sending Rose Smuin. 

Added you to the TG Technical Support so you can see the back and forth.

Good Morning,
We are tempering on both units, but when recirculating molds we have had 4 mold jams as of now on different spots.
-Before cold stamp 
-Conveyors after drum inserter
-After cooling cabinet 2
-Preheating Cabinet. 

Also, south swecos were installed upside down again.
Itzel Chavez were you able to get support from Theegarten? I had the tech looking to support this morning.
No yet, is he doing a teams call or something, or it’s via email?
Depositor 1 Centerlines checklist and shadow board
Depositor 2 Centerlines and checklist
Depositor 3 centerlines and checklist
Depositor 4 checklist
Ptls checklist and shadow board

Did somebody on your team reach out for request? I wasn't included on anything so I just asked the guy on site at the show to reach out to their service and let them know to be responsive when the request come through.
I believe Preston reached out.
I have reached out to Konstantin, haven’t received an answer yet.
We are depositing.
Preston did reach out at 11:34pm yesterday to make the request on the TG Technical chat. 👍
Depósitor 3 in the range

We are depositing 400 molds only,
Gondola will get full with around 300 molds, and will do 100 more to leave them in cooling cabinet 4 for testing.
Depositor 3 in the range

Itzel Chavez Chad Stone Rose Smuin Matt Hatch There is a teams call at 7am with TG. I'll have a headset out on the machines not in a conference room. Justin Halladay might be able to come over but he's dealing with a bad jam over there in L2. I'm currently going over a couple documents that Julia from TG sent me for the issues.
Lot end date is correct
Tempering chart check update
Are we working with the operator to bring that down?
Organoleptic check update

Thank you Itzel Chavez

Let’s also ensure we start up TG3 to know there are not any issues. 

The goal would be to 

- Have TG3 run 200-300 bars to know that it works

- Have the Gondola full so we can troubleshoot TG4 & TG5

- Have the WDS empty

This way as soon as Preston gets TG 4 or TG5 back to standard we start ML4 back up at full speed (it is empty so you don’t have to pause for anything)
weight check update
Yes
He has already made adjustments to the temperature.
Those look like they bloomed from that early temper spike. Do we have ee's watching and pulling them off until it goes away?

This adjustment sensor cable was broken off at the plug. Luckily the damage is minimal but this is something that will keep a flow wrapper down for hours if it was worse. Please have your teams be careful. Itzel Chavez Tyson Fausett Kimberly Morales Nathan Gevenois Rich Brierley Marcos Montano Alan Greer Rose Smuin
Dimensions check update
Yip: We already have a coworker doing that procedure with the bad chocolate.
On TG4, the button—which indicates where to make the cut—was switched off. The air was positioned too far back; the operator returned it to its proper place.

These changes had been made on Tg5; when the chocolate arrived, it began jamming before entering the TG. The operator switched the settings back to how we had left them on Friday, and it started packaging without any issues.
Thank you for the updates, Alba!☺️
TG4 working without any problems.
TG5 working without any problems.

Itzel Chavez Tyson Fausett  Theegarten 4 and 5 started up this morning with very little issues. I had their team on a conference call and they were ready to assist but none of the problems from Friday night were present.

Thank you for communicating Preston Elmer

Did the TG4 motor cause any more issues?

Itzel Chavez,

Have we started depositing at 25 MPM?
Yes we started at 7:30am depositing full speed 25mpm.

We are down due to the Gondola showing this alarm 

Preston Elmer is here
Packaging display box check update

Motor# S4M42
Had a loose roller. Preston is adjusting it.
This alarm it’s something with the pushers for TG #4, Preston is still troubleshooting.
1 of the gondola carriages is bent.
Preston said when it comes back around we can swap it for a good one.
Organoleptic check update The product's appearance looks much better.

Tempering chart check update 


The temperature of the enrober was regulated and falls within the parameters required by the centerline.
Which machine was this?
TG4
We are still down due to the Gondola, Preston is getting in contact with TG right now to troubleshoot.
That’s good to hear. The only thing that happened to TG4 on Friday night was the splitter over load at the end of the shift
TG#3 belt was not properly assembled

Still down,
TG is telling Preston to do a reference run on the Gondola, he’s waiting on instructions on how to do it.
Doing reference run now.
Back up and running at 10:20am.

Preston Elmer
will you give us an update of what the root cause was on this?

Down at 11:28am due to Gondola 

Same alarm.
We need to empty out the gondola and re zero it again.
We are back up and running.

I am not certain what made the gondola lose its position. But we now know how to zero it easily when it does. It has happened twice today. We noticed that each crash so far was on a carriage that had a bowed slat.

What causes the bowed slat? I talked to them today about replacing all of them with ones that don't have the corners cut off with a band saw. If there a structural issue with the slats as well that needs addressing?
Mold jam molding line 4 wds at 12:33 working on it
Mold jam again ML4  WDS 1:00 working on it
We are back and running ML4 12:37
We are back up and running ML4  WDS 1:04
Organoleptic check update
Tempering chart check
Weight check update
Dimensions check update
Packaging display box check update
ML4 is down due to issues with the gondola; the belt speed is not synchronized with the gondola, causing the chocolate to break upon entry. Maintenance is already working on the issue.
The gondola will be emptied to set it to 0
TG 5
We are synchronizing the Gondola
2nd shift deposit 4
Deposit 1 shift 2
We are emptying the chocolate out of the depositors then we will start
Countdown
Tg4
Tg3
Gondola is having the same issues with the bars hitting the trays and breaking them
The lot and the BB were verified
My team noticed during a purge that Depositor 1 is not pushing chocolate directly down on some of the nozzles. I am having my team remove the nozzle plate and sliders to clean them.
Centerlines deposit 3
Centerlines y temper deposit 4
We are emptying out the Gondola again so Maintenance can take another look
After looking at it the ramp is out of positing maintenance opened a door on the Gondola and which resets the position of the ramp

Good morning, the hopper heat was off and the chocolate in the hopper became hard/cold. I checked the shutdown checklist to verify if it had been completed, and it was signed off. Step 4 states to leave the hopper heat ON and set it to 120°F. It is important to carefully verify each shutdown step before signing off the checklist.
An adjustment was made to the mixing water, but the water jacket is not remaining stable; Maintenance has been called to inspect it.
We had one good Temper at 0.7. Took another temper and result was 1.1
The metal detector belt moved
Maintenance is working on it
Lot and date is correct
We are currently still waiting on temper and making adjustments as needed
Screen free of damage and clean
Robert Villarreal
On Chris's recommendation, the machine is set to Reheat at 7:30.
That's not reheat. That's night mode.
Starting reheat now.
metal detector belt is moved again
Maintenance fixed that
The recipe has started.
Update on action taken for temper since 6:02 am
Two good tempers have come out; we are waiting for a third temper to get started.
The metal detector is constantly activating and opening, the conveyor belt is moving. Maintenance has been checking it.
9:15 we started depositing
Maintenance is currently working on the metal detector
Maintenance continues working on the detector belt; if it does not function correctly, the line is stopped.
The metal detector is opening constantly, and maintenance is working on it.
We stopped the line because the belt is still moving.
Metal detector belt has shifted maintenance is making adjustments
We lowered the mixing water to 83.2
Let's empty the hopper
The band problem has not yet been resolved.
The temper is at 0.9, and the mixing water was lowered to 83.0 at 10:36.
@Robert maintenance is still working on the line should we place back into reheat
We lowered the mixing water to 82.8.
I am on my way over
Buffer tank temp is low. Raised it from 48 to 51 but soon I'll put it to 50 and keep it there. May need to update startup checklists

Found loose set screws as a probable cause as to why this pulley shaft shifted over and caused this mess on the metal detector belt. Realigned and tightened everything down. Operators are now cleaning the belt so there is no more metal dust.

Set buffer to 50 degrees. 


Action will be added in RCA regarding buffer temp
Currently we are still down due to metal detector issues. The belt has been cleaned and maintenance is working on the issue
Robert Villarreal Chad Stone Rose Smuin Rich Brierley We are still having Temper issues current Temper is 1.4. We get one good reading then out of spec
Rose Smuin Chad Stone Robert Villarreal Rich Brierley Still working on temper issues and maintenance is currently working on metal detector current temper is 0.9

At this point lets do another melting mode run until it gets to temperature if maintenance is still down. Adjustments don't seem to be working and while I was there we were making headway on temper but ultimately failed
Frederick/Grey,
Thoughts?
Robert Villarreal Frederick It says to put it on "melting."

Temper Update:  -0.7,  0.1,  0.2
Metal Detector Update: Still on progress.

Temper is holding steady for the most part. It’s a little concerning though that the outlet temp is around 85.9 but we’re getting slopes around .2, I feel like with that we should be closer to 0. Robert Villarrealdo you think I should make any changes right now?
Melissa Zavala please help with line clearance and monitor this area when production starts again
Thats great! Im walking in so let's look at it together Nathan
Line clearance is good so far! I will continue to monitor this throughout the shift
Line is running
The operator has just been informed to make adjustments, as the center pieces are arriving open at the bottom.
Thank you very much—the bottom part of the final piece has improved.
Justin Garner
The line started running at a speed of 65; right now, it has increased to 67.
In a cluster piece within the MMK Jars, QA found a piece containing metal; we have stopped the line to investigate the source of the metal.
I have just informed the supervisor that a piece of metal was found inside a jar that was rejected by the X-ray scanner. The action plan is to stop the line to conduct a follow-up investigation.
Does the metal detector reject it?

Maintenance work is currently being conducted in the metal detector area. As part of their investigation, test cards are being passed through the metal detector and are reading correctly; however, when the actual piece containing the detected material is passed through, it is not detected—leading them to conclude that the material cannot be metal.
No

The MD did not pick it up, the X-ray did rejected the jar.  

All maintenance cards were rejected at the MD. Charity Young can you come to S2 MD
Is it magnetic?
Viscosity, WA, Brix Caramel
Justin Garner approval to start the line back up. We will finalize investigation, and what material we will put on hold out of an abundance of caution.
Thank you!  The line is back up and running.
One of the rails for the checkweigher belt is not installed; we found it elsewhere. Please reinstall it once you have finished cleaning. Thank you.Brandon Hernandez Joseph Scott

Different shapes are detected differently on x ray vs metal detector. It could still be metal but the shape is incompatible with the sensitivity of the metal detector, particularly if the length of the item is traveling perpendicular to the detection field.

Maintenance has completed an inspection on the following equipment and found nothing out of the ordinary:
Salt Depositor, Cooling Tunnel 3, Evaporator Coils, Blowers, Eaglestone Conveyors, and Loma Checkweighers.

I removed the top Cap Full test eye and installed a new second Cap Full detection EYE 2 to the original one that I added in the past. I watched the capper run and it worked great for 2 hrs, so I will leave them installed.
  

To help with different cap size and stop positions the program detects full when either eye is blocked. The Elevator will start when both eyes are clear. (In the past the elevator would over run due to missed detection using only one eye).
On the Cap Accumulation after the Elevator I also added Stop bushings behind the Lexan to help prevent miss adjustment when going to the thinner lids. For thinner lids all they need to do is adjust inn to the stoppers. For larger lids turn all knobs an equal amount counter-clockwise.
Please update PRS job for tomorow to use 10200 instead of 10121 Sofia Carcamo
Sollich 2
1 shift
Good afternoon. We have a significant backlog of product—specifically at the X-ray stations, the lightweight container area, and the packing stations—where the line is moving at a very high speed.

Victor Romero Sr. Katya Farnsworth Justin Garner

This has been discussed a couple times now.  We need to be maintaining this area
Can we see the time stamp on those pouches please?
We are staging 2 people to help with the back log I believe it was the shift change that causing this
Ok, if that is the cases we need to work on how we make that more smooth
We have varying production rates, but we are also seeing many bags rejected due to underweight, which we have had to place in a container.

Due to issues presented on previous runs with belt from tunnel 3 slipping due to the excess of salt, an hourly check was added under key follow ups, for the Line leads LSW. This was part of the RCA actions. Justin Garner Dakota Adair Jody Howse please pass the info into your sups to make sure leads start using the updated LSW.
Hello team, as a friendly reminder, please don't forget not to over fill the trays with caramel. Thanks!

Good morning, team. We encountered a damaged chocolate flow valve—the one running from the buffer to the enrobers. We made the necessary adjustments, but it is still not functioning. We require assistance from the mechanics.
We have also secured the separator table, accompanied by a constant noise.
The guillotine isn't working.
We are waiting for maintenance to arrive.
The buffer was found full and overflowing.
Preston Elmer, Nathan Ruiz Nelson Cook
Clean buffer area
Right now, the mechanic is working on the valve.
The bottomer isn't working; the main scraper is likely down.
We need the Bottomer lever of the enrober to be completely closed because when it is opened, the main scraper falls immediately; for that reason, it is noted in the centerline
Lot and date is correct
Return line valve is now operational the buffer tank was at 107% and is currently being drained into the set points so the valve will work in auto mode.
Guillotine is now operational the servo drive was faulted out
Thank you Nathan Ruiz
The line started at 9:14am
What was the issue with the valve?

Matt Hatch
the buffer tank was at 107% it would not let it turn on.
But do we know why it went to 107%?
Pre  piece in the range
The temper reached 1.5, so we stopped the line.
Temper is currently at 1.9
Good first temper
Rose Smuin Preston Elmer Rich Brierley During removal of chocolate from the line a broken cover conveyor cover was found
Happy Monday 😊

These covers were a custom request to replace the big bulky fiberglass ones that keep breaking because they are so big and heavy. There is no air cooling on that tunnel, so it was really just to cover the product. But if QA would rather get rid of items that will break, we can just leave the conveyor uncovered. It shouldn't affect the product or functionality of the cooling belt.
We have 3 good continuous tempers

This pallet was left behind due to low weights. Instructions were to mix the low-WIP boxes with boxes that met acceptable weight standards. Will second shift have any issues in IMS using first-shift numbers?
If so, what is the best way for IMS to handle this?

Tyson Marcos Fredy Montalvan
This was resolved 👍
A different crack was discovered on a different cover for Tunnel 3. There is no loose plastic around the area. Lorena Abreu please help monitor the cracks and inform if it gets worse please?
the line started at 6:30

We are having problems with the combination of the matrix and the vibration of the scales, it is making many pauses and we have approximately 38 PPM but the machine speed is 43 PPM, maintenance is on the line
We received the C1 adjustment at 4.5, which caused the bag to fold when reaching the suction pickups. We set it to CL at 13.5 and it is working correctly.
Date and Lot is correct

We have been having problems with the buckets, the screen shows many errors, we have had several buckets open, the operator removes them and places them in the correct position but after a few minutes we have the problem again, the screen also shows errors on certain buckets but when checking them they are working correctly, this affects the amount being poured into the bag which sometimes results in several bags being re-injected
The vibration of the scales continues to be a problem, maintenance is on the line
we did the rezero and its working very well
After inspection I found 3 linear feeders set up like this which was causing the vibration faults.
We’re getting bad print paused clean and try making it better
Lines running issue was suction cup was too low causing the ink to smear
Water test results: pass ✔️
Is this on ML2 or ML3 Alex Mendoza?
A total of 749 pieces were counted. No empty wraps, doubles, or open wraps were found.

Line remains down. Temper unit, depositor, and mixer have been put in reheat until further notice.

Maintenance is pulling motor M117 as they think it is bad.

Once we determine how long it takes, we will try swapping with ML3 to test the motor to confirm it is bad.

Chris Givens, Chad Stone, Brandon Lance, Alex Mendoza

Aubrey Howe, Janell Scharrer, Maria Moron Quintero, Daniel Bringas - FYI this motor is on the Theegarten.

Maintenance switched the motor and it seemed to work. They quickly pulled the motor and put it back in ML3 and put the original motor back in ML2.

Once they powered everything on, the original motor started to work. As we started sending product, it shut down again.

We are pulling the motor and swapping with ML3 to see if the ML3 motor will run longer or fault out as we empty the cooler.

We will also try the ML2 motor on ML3 and see if we see the same issues on ML3 as ML2.

Chris Givens, Chad Stone, Brandon Lance, Dave Ruff

There is no spec of what the pre piece weight needs to be. Without the pre piece weight range, we are unable to define the chocolate usage range or target and the spec does not show it. However, I should be able to document the chocolate usage once we know the pre piece and final piece weights. There just won't be a spec of what the minimun/ maximum choc usage should be. When Candon and Rafael arrive, I will F2F with them on how much more 'trend' data is needed to assign a pretzel weight spec. Mary Montilla continue to gather pre-piece weights by taking a weight for every lot used, then immediately giving that weight information to production to adjust their chocolate usage to achieve the target 5.73g

Jay Alba do we have estop covers available?  Looks like we are missing a few of them

The turbotemper feeding pump went off due to the enrober not being ready at almost the same time the E-stop was pressed. There is an 11 minute difference in the enrober time and turbotemper time, which means that the feeding pump was disabled due to the enrober not being ready after the E-stop was pressed almost instantly. I believe that is what caused the system to froze since both returning pump and riser pump were disabled.
We are now tempering. Justin Katya
